---
title: "Comparison DLNM params"
output: 
  html_document:
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r, echo=FALSE, message = FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Load necessary libraries
library(readxl)     # reading Excel files
library(tidyverse)      # data manipulation
library(labelled)   # labeling columns 
library(here) # file paths
library(haven)
library(stargazer)
library(dlnm)
library(data.table)
library(forcats) # For reordering factor levels
library(broom)
library(MASS)
library(brant) # test proportional odds assumption for ordinal logistic regression
library(gt)
library(patchwork)
# source in code to load and clean data

## override mass::select and use dplyr as default
select <- dplyr::select 

source(here("data_cleaning", "clean_stress_data.R")) # pss_recode and crisis_recode and the output datasets of interest


# load selected temp data
era5 <- read_csv(here("data", "GRAPHS-ERA5-CHIRPS", "GRAPHS-ERA5-Heatstress-Tw-1991missing.csv")) %>%
  filter(date > as.Date("2013-01-01")) %>%
  mutate(community = case_when(community == "AKORA/AT" ~ "AKORA",
                               TRUE ~ community))

# load in precip data 
precip <- read_csv(here("data", "GRAPHS-ERA5-CHIRPS", "GRAPHS-CHIRPS-Precip.csv")) 

precip_clean <- precip %>%
  select(community, date, precip) %>%
  filter(date > as.Date("2013-01-01")) %>%
  mutate(date = lubridate::ymd(date),
         month = lubridate::month(date))%>%
  mutate(community = case_when(community == "AKORA/AT" ~ "AKORA",
                               TRUE ~ community))

# check definition of rainy season 
precip_clean_summary <- precip_clean %>%
  group_by(month) %>%
  summarise(total_precip = sum(precip))

# load selected temp data
WBGT_daily_max <- era5 %>%
  dplyr::select(community, date, max_wbgt)  # select relevant columns

WBGT_daily_mean <- era5 %>%
  dplyr::select(community, date, mean_wbgt)  # select relevant columns

WBGT_daily_min <- era5 %>%
  dplyr::select(community, date, min_wbgt)  # select relevant columns

```


## Sum NDS Category


```{r, echo=FALSE, message = FALSE, warning=FALSE}
### LOAD THE CRYSIS DATA

## CRYSIS data
crisis_simple <- crisis_recode %>%
  mutate(
    quessetdt = sprintf("%04d", quessetdt),  # Ensure quassetdt has 4 digits
    quessetdt = sub("(\\d{2})(\\d{2})", "\\1:\\2", quessetdt),  # Insert colon
    survey_datetime = as.POSIXct(paste(quessetd, quessetdt), format = "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M")) %>%
    select(mstudyid, fin_events:survey_datetime, datdeliv:survey_pre_post_birth, gestage_days, date_conception, married, wealthindex, age, bmi, medlev, fan, pregchn) %>%
  select(-c(crireadneg:crikidneg)) %>%
  mutate(month_delivery = lubridate::month(datdeliv),
         season_delivery = case_when(month_delivery %in% c(4,5,6,7,8,9,10) ~ "wet", 
                                     month_delivery %in% c(1,2,3,11,12) ~"dry",
                                     TRUE ~ NA)) %>%
  mutate(survey_date = as.Date(survey_datetime))  # Extract the date part of survey_datetime



```

### T

```{r, echo=FALSE, message = FALSE, warning=FALSE}
## Create Daily Lag Frame:
max_gestation_days <- 210
pre_conception_days <- 30

crisis_dlnm_eligible <- crisis_simple %>% 
  filter(gestage_days >= max_gestation_days) # ~37 weeks


# Function to process temperature data for the selected metric
process_temperature_data <- function(era5_data, metric) {
  era5_data %>%
    select(community, date, !!sym(metric)) %>%
    rename(temp_metric = !!sym(metric)) %>%
    distinct()
}

# Process temp data
temp_data_clean <- process_temperature_data(era5_data = era5, metric = "max_wbgt")



# Generate lags relative to conception
create_conception_aligned_lags <- function(df, temp_df) {
  df %>%
    select(mstudyid, vname, date_conception) %>%
    rowwise() %>%
    mutate(day_relative = list(-pre_conception_days:max_gestation_days),
           date = list(date_conception + day_relative)) %>%
    unnest(c(day_relative, date)) %>%
    left_join(temp_df, by = c("vname" = "community", "date" = "date"))  # join temp
}


temp_lags_long <- create_conception_aligned_lags(crisis_dlnm_eligible, temp_data_clean)

# Step 4: Pivot to wide format (like DLNM expects)
temperature_lags_wide <- temp_lags_long %>%
  select(mstudyid, date_conception, day_relative, temp_metric) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = day_relative,
    values_from = temp_metric,
    names_prefix = "day_"
  ) %>%
  left_join(crisis_dlnm_eligible %>% select(mstudyid, sum_nds_category, age, bmi, wealthindex, season_delivery), by = "mstudyid") %>%
  filter(!is.na(day_0))

temp_data_matrix <- temperature_lags_wide %>%
  select(starts_with("day_")) %>%
  as.matrix()

```


## function to examine BIC for range of models to optimize DLNM parameters

```{r, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
## Create a grid-search function for ordinal logistic regression (polr)
fit_DLNMs_polr <- function(args_list, exposure_data, min_lag, max_lag,
                           model_data, outcome, covars, verbose = TRUE) {
  
  num_args <- length(args_list)
  combos <- expand.grid(var = 1:num_args, lag = 1:num_args)
  
  results <- tibble(
    DLNM_argvar = character(),
    DLNM_arglag = character(),
    AIC = numeric(),
    BIC = numeric()
  )
  
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(combos))) {
    var_i <- combos$var[i]
    lag_i <- combos$lag[i]
    
    cb <- crossbasis(
      exposure_data,
      lag = c(min_lag, max_lag),
      argvar = args_list[[var_i]],
      arglag = args_list[[lag_i]]
    )
    
    formula_str <- paste(outcome, "~ cb +", paste(covars, collapse = " + "))
    model <- polr(as.formula(formula_str), data = model_data, method = "logistic")
    
    results <- add_row(
      results,
      DLNM_argvar = paste(names(args_list[[var_i]]), args_list[[var_i]], collapse = ", "),
      DLNM_arglag = paste(names(args_list[[lag_i]]), args_list[[lag_i]], collapse = ", "),
      AIC = AIC(model),
      BIC = BIC(model)
    )
  }
  
  best_BIC <- results %>% filter(BIC == min(BIC, na.rm = TRUE))
  best_AIC <- results %>% filter(AIC == min(AIC, na.rm = TRUE))
  
  if (verbose) {
    cat("\nLowest BIC model:\n")
    print(best_BIC)
    cat("\nLowest AIC model:\n")
    print(best_AIC)
  }
  
  list(results = results, best_BIC = best_BIC, best_AIC = best_AIC)
}
```

### Run the search

```{r, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
## Define the parameter grid 
args <- list(
  list(fun = "bs", df = 3, degree = 2),
  list(fun = "bs", df = 4, degree = 2),
  list(fun = "bs", df = 4, degree = 3),
  list(fun = "bs", df = 5, degree = 2),
  list(fun = "bs", df = 5, degree = 3),
  list(fun = "bs", df = 5, degree = 4),
  list(fun = "bs", df = 6, degree = 3),
  list(fun = "bs", df = 6, degree = 4),
  list(fun = "bs", df = 6, degree = 5)
)

dlnm_search <- fit_DLNMs_polr(
  args_list = args,
  exposure_data = temp_data_matrix,
  min_lag = 0,
  max_lag = ncol(temp_data_matrix) - 1,
  model_data = temperature_lags_wide,
  outcome = "sum_nds_category",
  covars = c("age", "bmi", "wealthindex", "season_delivery")
)


dlnm_search$results %>%
  arrange(BIC)


# try again with natural spline
# Define natural spline options (df indicates knots are used)
ns_args <- list(
  list(fun = "ns", df = 2),
  list(fun = "ns", df = 3),
  list(fun = "ns", df = 4),
  list(fun = "ns", df = 5)
)

# Run DLNM parameter search with natural spline basis
dlnm_search_ns <- fit_DLNMs_polr(
  args_list = ns_args,
  exposure_data = temp_data_matrix,
  min_lag = 0,
  max_lag = ncol(temp_data_matrix) - 1,
  model_data = temperature_lags_wide,
  outcome = "sum_nds_category",
  covars = c("age", "bmi", "wealthindex", "season_delivery")
)

dlnm_search_ns$results %>%
  arrange(BIC)

```


### function to plot contours 

```{r}
plot_dlnm_model <- function(var_fun, var_df, lag_fun, lag_df, 
                            temp_data_matrix, data_df, outcome, covars) {

  cb <- crossbasis(
    temp_data_matrix,
    lag   = c(0, ncol(temp_data_matrix) - 1),
    argvar = list(fun = var_fun, df = var_df),
    arglag = list(fun = lag_fun, df = lag_df)
  )

  f <- as.formula(paste(outcome, "~ cb +", paste(covars, collapse = " + ")))
  model <- MASS::polr(f, data = data_df, method = "logistic", Hess = TRUE)

  temp_median <- round(median(as.vector(temp_data_matrix)), 1)

  ## Let crosspred extract coef/vcov from the polr model:
  cpred <- dlnm::crosspred(cb, model, cen = temp_median, ci.level = 0.95, by = 0.1)

  plot(cpred, "contour",
       main = paste0("DLNM (", var_fun, " df=", var_df, ", ", lag_fun, " df=", lag_df, ")\n",
                     "Relative to median ", temp_median, "°C"),
       xlab = "Temperature (°C)", ylab = "Days relative to conception",
       key.title = title(main = "Change in\nLog Odds", cex.main = 0.8))

  invisible(list(model = model, cpred = cpred))
}


# PLOTS OF TOP MODELS
plot_dlnm_model("ns", 2, "ns", 2, temp_data_matrix, temperature_lags_wide,
                "sum_nds_category", c("age","bmi","wealthindex","season_delivery")) # oversimplifies

plot_dlnm_model("ns", 4, "ns", 2, temp_data_matrix, temperature_lags_wide,
                "sum_nds_category", c("age","bmi","wealthindex","season_delivery"))

plot_dlnm_model("ns", 4, "ns", 3, temp_data_matrix, temperature_lags_wide,
                "sum_nds_category", c("age","bmi","wealthindex","season_delivery"))

plot_dlnm_model("ns", 5, "ns", 2, temp_data_matrix, temperature_lags_wide,
                "sum_nds_category", c("age","bmi","wealthindex","season_delivery"))

```



```{r}
library(MASS)
library(dlnm)
library(tidyverse)

# Fit one DLNM and return cpred + label
fit_cpred <- function(var_df, lag_df, var_fun = "ns", lag_fun = "ns",
                      temp_data_matrix, data_df, outcome, covars,
                      center = c("median","tmin")) {
  center <- match.arg(center)

  cb <- crossbasis(
    temp_data_matrix,
    lag   = c(0, ncol(temp_data_matrix) - 1),
    argvar = list(fun = var_fun, df = var_df),
    arglag = list(fun = lag_fun, df = lag_df)
  )

  f <- as.formula(paste(outcome, "~ cb +", paste(covars, collapse = " + ")))
  model <- MASS::polr(f, data = data_df, method = "logistic", Hess = TRUE)

  # choose centering
  temp_vec <- as.vector(temp_data_matrix)
  if (center == "median") {
    cen_val <- round(median(temp_vec), 1)
  } else {
    # Tmin (temp of minimum predicted risk) using a quick median-centered cpred
    tmp <- crosspred(cb, model, cen = round(median(temp_vec), 1), by = 0.1)
    cen_val <- as.numeric(names(which.min(tmp$allRRfit)))
  }

  cpred <- crosspred(cb, model, cen = cen_val, ci.level = 0.95, by = 0.1)

  list(
  label = sprintf("df = %d, lag = %d", var_df, lag_df),
  var_df = var_df, lag_df = lag_df,
  model = model, cb = cb, cpred = cpred, cen = cen_val
)
}


center_suffix <- function(fits) {
  cents <- vapply(fits, function(x) x$cen, numeric(1))
  cents_u <- unique(round(cents, 1))
  if (length(cents_u) == 1) {
    sprintf(" (center = %.1f°C)", cents_u)
  } else {
    sprintf(" (centers = %s°C)", paste(format(cents_u, nsmall = 1), collapse = ", "))
  }
}

# Helper: safely pull a row from cpred at a temperature value,
# falling back to the nearest available temp if exact match is absent
extract_temp_row <- function(cpred, target_temp) {
  temps <- as.numeric(rownames(cpred$matfit))
  idx <- which.min(abs(temps - target_temp))
  list(idx = idx, temp = temps[idx])
}



# Build lag-slice data for one cpred at one temperature percentile
lag_slice_df <- function(cpred, percentile, temp_data_matrix, label){
  target <- quantile(as.vector(temp_data_matrix), percentile/100)
  take <- extract_temp_row(cpred, round(as.numeric(target), 1))

  fit  <- cpred$matfit[take$idx, ]
  low  <- cpred$matlow[take$idx, ]
  high <- cpred$mathigh[take$idx, ]

  tibble(
    lag = as.numeric(gsub("lag", "", names(fit))),
    effect = as.numeric(fit),
    ci_low = as.numeric(low),
    ci_high = as.numeric(high),
    model = label,
    temp_value = take$temp,
    percentile = percentile
  )
}

ordinal <- function(n){
  n <- as.integer(n)
  suffix <- ifelse(
    n %% 100L %in% c(11L, 12L, 13L),
    "th",
    c("th", "st", "nd", "rd", "th")[pmin(5, (n %% 10L) + 1)]
  )
  paste0(n, suffix)
}

# OVERLAY plots for multiple models at chosen percentiles
plot_lag_slices_across_models <- function(fits, temp_data_matrix,
                                          percentiles = c(1, 25, 75, 99),
                                          main_prefix = "Lag-response at temp percentiles",
                                          ylims = NULL,
                                          legend_top = TRUE) {
  # gather all slices
  all <- purrr::map_dfr(fits, function(x) {
    purrr::map_dfr(percentiles, ~ lag_slice_df(x$cpred, .x, temp_data_matrix, x$label))
  })

  if (is.null(ylims)) {
    ylims <- range(c(all$ci_low, all$ci_high), finite = TRUE)
  }

  title_text <- paste0(main_prefix, center_suffix(fits))

  
  p <- all %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      panel = factor(paste0(ordinal(percentile), " (", sprintf("%.1f", temp_value), "°C)"), levels = unique(paste0(
        ordinal(percentiles),
        " (",
        sprintf("%.1f", purrr::map_dbl(
          percentiles, ~ stats::quantile(as.vector(temp_data_matrix), .x / 100)
        )),
        "°C)"
      )))
    ) %>%
    ggplot2::ggplot(ggplot2::aes(lag, effect, color = model, fill = model)) +
    ggplot2::geom_ribbon(ggplot2::aes(ymin = ci_low, ymax = ci_high), alpha = 0.15, color = NA) +
    ggplot2::geom_line(size = 1) +
    ggplot2::facet_wrap(~ panel, scales = "fixed") +
    ggplot2::geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = 2) +
    ggplot2::coord_cartesian(ylim = ylims) +
    ggplot2::labs(x = "Days relative to conception (0 = 30 days before)",
                  y = "Change in log-odds (vs. center)", color = "Model", fill = "Model") +
    ggplot2::theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

  if (legend_top) {
    p <- p +
      labs(color = "Spline specification", fill = "Spline specification") +
      theme(
        legend.position = "top",
        legend.box = "horizontal",
        legend.text = element_text(size = 11),
        legend.title = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
        legend.key.width = unit(1.2, "cm"),
        legend.spacing.x = unit(0.3, "cm"),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 11.5),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 12.5)
      ) +
      guides(color = guide_legend(nrow = 1, byrow = TRUE),
             fill  = guide_legend(nrow = 1, byrow = TRUE))
  }
  p
}



# ggplot-friendly exposure slices produced by dlnm's slicer
exposure_slice_df <- function(cpred, lag_value, label){
  # dlnm's base plotter computes slices internally, but we can emulate via $allRR...
  # Here we call dlnm's base slicer, capture values, or reconstruct from cpred fields.
  # Simpler approach: use the "slices" method to compute values and capture via grDevices.
  # Instead, we rebuild from cpred$matfit columns = lags; rows = temperatures:
  lags <- as.numeric(gsub("lag", "", colnames(cpred$matfit)))
  j <- which.min(abs(lags - lag_value))  # nearest lag column

  tibble(
    temp = as.numeric(rownames(cpred$matfit)),
    effect = cpred$matfit[, j],
    ci_low = cpred$matlow[, j],
    ci_high = cpred$mathigh[, j],
    model = label,
    lag = lags[j]
  )
}

plot_exposure_slices_across_models <- function(
  fits, lags = c(0, 7, 30, 100, 200),
  main_prefix = "Exposure-response at fixed lags",
  ylims = NULL,
  legend_top = TRUE,
  free_y = FALSE
) {
  all <- purrr::map_dfr(fits, function(x) {
    purrr::map_dfr(lags, ~ exposure_slice_df(x$cpred, .x, x$label))
  })

  # global y limits only if we're NOT using free_y
  if (!free_y) {
    if (is.null(ylims)) ylims <- range(c(all$ci_low, all$ci_high), finite = TRUE)
  }

  title_text <- paste0(main_prefix, center_suffix(fits))

  p <- all %>%
    dplyr::mutate(panel = factor(paste0("Lag ", lag, " d"),
                                 levels = paste0("Lag ", sort(unique(all$lag)), " d"))) %>%
    ggplot2::ggplot(ggplot2::aes(temp, effect, color = model, fill = model)) +
    ggplot2::geom_ribbon(ggplot2::aes(ymin = ci_low, ymax = ci_high), alpha = 0.15, color = NA) +
    ggplot2::geom_line(size = 1) +
    ggplot2::facet_wrap(~ panel, scales = if (free_y) "free_y" else "fixed") +
    ggplot2::geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = 2) +
    ggplot2::labs(y = "Change in log-odds (vs. center)", 
                  x = "Daily maximum WBGT (°C)",
                  color = "Model", 
                  fill = "Model") +
    ggplot2::theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

  if (!free_y) {
    p <- p + ggplot2::coord_cartesian(ylim = ylims)
  }

  if (legend_top) {
    p <- p +
      labs(color = "Spline specification", fill = "Spline specification") +
      theme(
        legend.position = "top",
        legend.box = "horizontal",
        legend.text = element_text(size = 11),
        legend.title = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
        legend.key.width = unit(1.2, "cm"),
        legend.spacing.x = unit(0.3, "cm"),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 11.5),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 12.5)
      ) +
      guides(color = guide_legend(nrow = 1, byrow = TRUE),
             fill  = guide_legend(nrow = 1, byrow = TRUE))
  }
  p
}


```


```{r}
# Build cpred objects for the four models (center at median for comparability)
fits <- list(
  fit_cpred(3, 3, temp_data_matrix = temp_data_matrix,
            data_df = temperature_lags_wide,
            outcome = "sum_nds_category",
            covars = c("age","bmi","wealthindex","season_delivery"),
            center = "median"),
  # fit_cpred(5, 4, temp_data_matrix = temp_data_matrix,
  #           data_df = temperature_lags_wide,
  #           outcome = "sum_nds_category",
  #           covars = c("age","bmi","wealthindex","season_delivery"),
  #           center = "median"),
  fit_cpred(4, 3, temp_data_matrix = temp_data_matrix,
            data_df = temperature_lags_wide,
            outcome = "sum_nds_category",
            covars = c("age","bmi","wealthindex","season_delivery"),
            center = "median"),
  fit_cpred(5, 3, temp_data_matrix = temp_data_matrix,
            data_df = temperature_lags_wide,
            outcome = "sum_nds_category",
            covars = c("age","bmi","wealthindex","season_delivery"),
            center = "median"),
  fit_cpred(4, 4, temp_data_matrix = temp_data_matrix,
            data_df = temperature_lags_wide,
            outcome = "sum_nds_category",
            covars = c("age","bmi","wealthindex","season_delivery"),
            center = "median")
)

# Choose a common y-limit across all models for fair visual comparison
# (you can widen/narrow this after seeing first render)
lag_plot <- plot_lag_slices_across_models(fits, temp_data_matrix,
                                          percentiles = c(1,25,75,99))

exposure_plot <- plot_exposure_slices_across_models(
  fits, lags = c(7, 30, 90, 150, 200, 275),
  main_prefix = "Exposure-response at fixed lags",
  free_y = TRUE
)



final_sensitivity_plot <-
  (lag_plot / exposure_plot) +                    # combine first
  plot_annotation(
    tag_levels = 'A',                             # auto-tags A, B
    #title = "Spline sensitivity analysis for Max WBGT",
    theme = theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14, hjust = 0.5),
      plot.tag = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12)  # style A/B
    )
  )

# Save Figure S1 (lag-response)
ggsave(
  filename = "S1_max_WBGT_sensitivity_new.png",
  plot = final_sensitivity_plot,
  path = here("outputs"),
  width =11,                 # in inches
  height = 8,
  dpi = 600,                 # high resolution for print/PDF
  bg = "white"
)

ggsave(
  filename = "Figure_S1_new.pdf",
  plot = final_sensitivity_plot,
  path = here("outputs"),  
  width = 11,
  height = 9,
  bg = "white"
)



```

## update titles

```{r}
lag_plot_final <- lag_plot +
  labs(title = "Figure S1. Lag–response Functions at Temperature Percentiles for Alternative Spline Specifications.",
       caption = "Curves show the change in log-odds of higher stress relative to 27.4 °C, across gestation. The comparison demonstrates that the ns(4) × ns(3) \nmodel best captures meaningful lag structure without overfitting.")


exposure_plot_final <- exposure_plot +
  labs(title ="Figure S2. Exposure–response Functions at Fixed Lags for the Same Spline Specifications.",
       caption = "Curves show the change in log-odds of higher stress relative to 27.4 °C at selected lag days. Y-axis scales vary by panel to emphasize curve shape; \nmagnitudes should be compared within, not across, panels. These results complement Figure S1, showing consistent exposure–response patterns \nacross spline choices, with the ns(4) × ns(3) model providing balanced flexibility.")



# Save Figure S1 (lag-response)
ggsave(
  filename = "max_WBGT_sensitivity_lag_response.png",
  plot = lag_plot,
  path = here("outputs"),  
  width =11,                 # in inches
  height = 6,
  dpi = 600,                 # high resolution for print/PDF
  bg = "white"
)

# Save Figure S2 (exposure-response)
ggsave(
  filename = "max_WBGT_sensitivity_exposure_response.png",
  plot = exposure_plot,
  path = here("outputs"),
  width = 11,
  height = 6,
  dpi = 600,
  bg = "white"
)

```


### Min WBGT

```{r, echo=FALSE, message = FALSE, warning=FALSE}
## Create Daily Lag Frame:
max_gestation_days <- 210
pre_conception_days <- 30

crisis_dlnm_eligible <- crisis_simple %>% 
  filter(gestage_days >= max_gestation_days) # ~37 weeks


# Function to process temperature data for the selected metric
process_temperature_data <- function(era5_data, metric) {
  era5_data %>%
    select(community, date, !!sym(metric)) %>%
    rename(temp_metric = !!sym(metric)) %>%
    distinct()
}

# Process temp data
temp_data_clean <- process_temperature_data(era5_data = era5, metric = "min_wbgt")



# Generate lags relative to conception
create_conception_aligned_lags <- function(df, temp_df) {
  df %>%
    select(mstudyid, vname, date_conception) %>%
    rowwise() %>%
    mutate(day_relative = list(-pre_conception_days:max_gestation_days),
           date = list(date_conception + day_relative)) %>%
    unnest(c(day_relative, date)) %>%
    left_join(temp_df, by = c("vname" = "community", "date" = "date"))  # join temp
}


temp_lags_long <- create_conception_aligned_lags(crisis_dlnm_eligible, temp_data_clean)

# Step 4: Pivot to wide format (like DLNM expects)
temperature_lags_wide <- temp_lags_long %>%
  select(mstudyid, date_conception, day_relative, temp_metric) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = day_relative,
    values_from = temp_metric,
    names_prefix = "day_"
  ) %>%
  left_join(crisis_dlnm_eligible %>% select(mstudyid, sum_nds_category, age, bmi, wealthindex, season_delivery), by = "mstudyid") %>%
  filter(!is.na(day_0))

temp_data_matrix <- temperature_lags_wide %>%
  select(starts_with("day_")) %>%
  as.matrix()

```


## function to examine BIC for range of models to optimize DLNM parameters

```{r, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
## Create a grid-search function for ordinal logistic regression (polr)
fit_DLNMs_polr <- function(args_list, exposure_data, min_lag, max_lag,
                           model_data, outcome, covars, verbose = TRUE) {
  
  num_args <- length(args_list)
  combos <- expand.grid(var = 1:num_args, lag = 1:num_args)
  
  results <- tibble(
    DLNM_argvar = character(),
    DLNM_arglag = character(),
    AIC = numeric(),
    BIC = numeric()
  )
  
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(combos))) {
    var_i <- combos$var[i]
    lag_i <- combos$lag[i]
    
    cb <- crossbasis(
      exposure_data,
      lag = c(min_lag, max_lag),
      argvar = args_list[[var_i]],
      arglag = args_list[[lag_i]]
    )
    
    formula_str <- paste(outcome, "~ cb +", paste(covars, collapse = " + "))
    model <- polr(as.formula(formula_str), data = model_data, method = "logistic")
    
    results <- add_row(
      results,
      DLNM_argvar = paste(names(args_list[[var_i]]), args_list[[var_i]], collapse = ", "),
      DLNM_arglag = paste(names(args_list[[lag_i]]), args_list[[lag_i]], collapse = ", "),
      AIC = AIC(model),
      BIC = BIC(model)
    )
  }
  
  best_BIC <- results %>% filter(BIC == min(BIC, na.rm = TRUE))
  best_AIC <- results %>% filter(AIC == min(AIC, na.rm = TRUE))
  
  if (verbose) {
    cat("\nLowest BIC model:\n")
    print(best_BIC)
    cat("\nLowest AIC model:\n")
    print(best_AIC)
  }
  
  list(results = results, best_BIC = best_BIC, best_AIC = best_AIC)
}
```

### Run the search

```{r, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
## Define the parameter grid 
args <- list(
  list(fun = "bs", df = 3, degree = 2),
  list(fun = "bs", df = 4, degree = 2),
  list(fun = "bs", df = 4, degree = 3),
  list(fun = "bs", df = 5, degree = 2),
  list(fun = "bs", df = 5, degree = 3),
  list(fun = "bs", df = 5, degree = 4),
  list(fun = "bs", df = 6, degree = 3),
  list(fun = "bs", df = 6, degree = 4),
  list(fun = "bs", df = 6, degree = 5)
)

dlnm_search <- fit_DLNMs_polr(
  args_list = args,
  exposure_data = temp_data_matrix,
  min_lag = 0,
  max_lag = ncol(temp_data_matrix) - 1,
  model_data = temperature_lags_wide,
  outcome = "sum_nds_category",
  covars = c("age", "bmi", "wealthindex", "season_delivery")
)


dlnm_search$results %>%
  arrange(BIC)


# try again with natural spline
# Define natural spline options (df indicates knots are used)
ns_args <- list(
  list(fun = "ns", df = 2),
  list(fun = "ns", df = 3),
  list(fun = "ns", df = 4),
  list(fun = "ns", df = 5)
)

# Run DLNM parameter search with natural spline basis
dlnm_search_ns <- fit_DLNMs_polr(
  args_list = ns_args,
  exposure_data = temp_data_matrix,
  min_lag = 0,
  max_lag = ncol(temp_data_matrix) - 1,
  model_data = temperature_lags_wide,
  outcome = "sum_nds_category",
  covars = c("age", "bmi", "wealthindex", "season_delivery")
)

dlnm_search_ns$results %>%
  arrange(BIC)

```


```{r}
# Build cpred objects for the four models (center at median for comparability)


plot_exposure_slices_across_models <- function(
  fits, lags = c(0, 7, 30, 100, 200),
  main_prefix = "Exposure-response at fixed lags",
  ylims = NULL,
  legend_top = TRUE,
  free_y = FALSE
) {
  all <- purrr::map_dfr(fits, function(x) {
    purrr::map_dfr(lags, ~ exposure_slice_df(x$cpred, .x, x$label))
  })

  # global y limits only if we're NOT using free_y
  if (!free_y) {
    if (is.null(ylims)) ylims <- range(c(all$ci_low, all$ci_high), finite = TRUE)
  }

  title_text <- paste0(main_prefix, center_suffix(fits))

  p <- all %>%
    dplyr::mutate(panel = factor(paste0("Lag ", lag, " d"),
                                 levels = paste0("Lag ", sort(unique(all$lag)), " d"))) %>%
    ggplot2::ggplot(ggplot2::aes(temp, effect, color = model, fill = model)) +
    ggplot2::geom_ribbon(ggplot2::aes(ymin = ci_low, ymax = ci_high), alpha = 0.15, color = NA) +
    ggplot2::geom_line(size = 1) +
    ggplot2::facet_wrap(~ panel, scales = if (free_y) "free_y" else "fixed") +
    ggplot2::geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = 2) +
    ggplot2::labs(y = "Change in log-odds (vs. center)", 
                  x = "Daily minimum WBGT (°C)",
                  color = "Model", 
                  fill = "Model") +
    ggplot2::theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

  if (!free_y) {
    p <- p + ggplot2::coord_cartesian(ylim = ylims)
  }

  if (legend_top) {
    p <- p +
      labs(color = "Spline specification", fill = "Spline specification") +
      theme(
        legend.position = "top",
        legend.box = "horizontal",
        legend.text = element_text(size = 11),
        legend.title = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
        legend.key.width = unit(1.2, "cm"),
        legend.spacing.x = unit(0.3, "cm"),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 11.5),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 12.5)
      ) +
      guides(color = guide_legend(nrow = 1, byrow = TRUE),
             fill  = guide_legend(nrow = 1, byrow = TRUE))
  }
  p
}



fits <- list(
  fit_cpred(3, 3, temp_data_matrix = temp_data_matrix,
            data_df = temperature_lags_wide,
            outcome = "sum_nds_category",
            covars = c("age","bmi","wealthindex","season_delivery"),
            center = "median"),
  fit_cpred(4, 3, temp_data_matrix = temp_data_matrix,
            data_df = temperature_lags_wide,
            outcome = "sum_nds_category",
            covars = c("age","bmi","wealthindex","season_delivery"),
            center = "median"),
  fit_cpred(5, 3, temp_data_matrix = temp_data_matrix,
            data_df = temperature_lags_wide,
            outcome = "sum_nds_category",
            covars = c("age","bmi","wealthindex","season_delivery"),
            center = "median"),
  fit_cpred(4, 4, temp_data_matrix = temp_data_matrix,
            data_df = temperature_lags_wide,
            outcome = "sum_nds_category",
            covars = c("age","bmi","wealthindex","season_delivery"),
            center = "median")
)

# Choose a common y-limit across all models for fair visual comparison
# (you can widen/narrow this after seeing first render)
lag_plot <- plot_lag_slices_across_models(fits, temp_data_matrix,
                                          percentiles = c(1,25,75,99))

exposure_plot <- plot_exposure_slices_across_models(
  fits, lags = c(7, 30, 90, 150, 200, 275),
  main_prefix = "Exposure-response at fixed lags",
  free_y = TRUE
)


final_sensitivity_plot <-
  (lag_plot / exposure_plot) +                    # combine first
  plot_annotation(
    tag_levels = 'A',                             # auto-tags A, B
    #title = "Spline sensitivity analysis for Min WBGT",
    theme = theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14, hjust = 0.5),
      plot.tag = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12)  # style A/B
    )
  )

## pdf save
ggsave(
  filename = "Figure_S2_new.pdf",
  plot = final_sensitivity_plot,
  path = here("outputs"),  
  width = 11,
  height = 9,
  bg = "white"
)
# 
# 
# # Save Figure S1 (lag-response)
# ggsave(
#   filename = "min_WBGT_sensitivity.png",
#   plot = final_sensitivity_plot,
#   path = here("outputs"),  
#   width =11,                 # in inches
#   height = 8,
#   dpi = 600,                 # high resolution for print/PDF
#   bg = "white"
# )
# 
# 
# # Save Figure S1 (lag-response)
# ggsave(
#   filename = "min_WBGT_sensitivity_lag_response.png",
#   plot = lag_plot,
#   path = here("outputs"),  
#   width =11,                 # in inches
#   height = 6,
#   dpi = 600,                 # high resolution for print/PDF
#   bg = "white"
# )
# 
# # Save Figure S2 (exposure-response)
# ggsave(
#   filename = "min_WBGT_sensitivity_exposure_response.png",
#   plot = exposure_plot,
#   path = here("outputs"),
#   width = 11,
#   height = 6,
#   dpi = 600,
#   bg = "white"
# )
```
